docs.summary: Provides functionality for elements that can be used and understood by as many people as possible, regardless of their abilities.
description: The `accessibility` object allows you to design your elements to be inclusive and usable by everyone including users with visial, auditory, motor, and cognitive disabilities. The accessibility object's attributes enhance the accessibility of Wix elements, especially for those who use screen readers and other assistive technologies.

Accessibility attributes include Accessible Rich Internet Applications (ARIA) attributes, specifically created for screen readers and users who can't use a mouse and rely on their keyboard. Learn more about [ARIA attributes](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA).
> The accessibility object only affects how assistive technologies read and present the content to users. It doesn't change any functionality or styling of your elements.

params.name: eventHandler
params.doc:
The name of the function or
 the function expression to run when a key is pressed.

ret.doc: The element on which the event is now registered.

docs.summary: Adds an event handler that runs when a keyboard key is pressed while the container or one of its child elements is focused.
description: A container receives a `keyPress` event when a site visitor presses a keyboard key on
the container or any of its child elements. A keyboard event is triggered for any child elements inside the container that support `onKeyPress()`.
A `keyPress` event is fired for printable and non-printable characters.
You can also [define an event handler using the Properties and Events panel](https://support.wix.com/en/article/velo-reacting-to-user-actions-using-events).
> + Some browsers don't issue a key press event for certain keys,
>   such as arrow keys or the shift key.
> + When you retrieve the `value` from the `target` property of a [`KeyboardEvent`]($w.KeyboardEvent.html),
>   you get the value of the target element before the key was pressed.

examples.title: Get the key that was pressed
Get all of the keyboard event's properties

docs.summary: An object containing attributes to make your Wix elements more accessible.

members.name: role
members.doc:
Defines the type of an element, helping assistive technologies understand the element's purpose. 

Supported roles: `'region'`, `'group'`, `'alert'`, `'status'`, `'radio'`, `'radioGroup'`, `'range'`, `'tab'`, `'tabList'`, `'tabPanel'`, `'button'`, `'link'`, `'application'`, `'none'`, `'list'`, `'listItem'`, `'listBox'`, `'option'`, `'checkbox'`, `'navigation'`, `'complementary'`, `'undefined'`, `'null'`. 

Default: `'undefined'`

members.name: ariaHidden
members.doc: Indicates whether the element is visible to or hidden from users that rely on assisive technologies. When set to `true`, the element is hidden from assistive technologies. The element is not affected visually on your site.

members.name: ariaRoleDescription
members.doc: Provides a custom text description for a role assigned to an element. This helps assistive technologies interpret the purpose of the element more accurately than `role`. For example, if your site has a custom button that acts as a "Favorite" button, you can specify the `ariaRoleDescription` as "Favorite Button" which overrides the generic value assigned to `role`.

members.name: ariaLabeledBy
members.doc:
Defines the accessible name of an element by referencing other elements by ID. Although `ariaLabeledBy` has the same functionality as `ariaLabel`, the accessible name set by `ariaLabeledBy` takes precedence. 

`$w(“#box2”).accessibility.ariaLabeledBy = $w(“#title3”);`

members.name: ariaDescribedBy
members.doc:
Provides a text description for an element by referencing other elements by ID. 

`$w(“#button2”).accessibility.ariaDescribedby = $w(“#title3”);`

members.name: ariaControls
members.doc:
A list of element IDs whose contents or presence are controlled by the element on which the attribute is set. This attribute helps screen readers and other assistive technologies understand the relationship between two or more elements. 

`$w(“#openPopupButton”).accessibility.ariaControls = [$w(“#popupBox1”)];`
